top10.introHtml: Top 10 best buys for a cheap, fun second car near Media, PA — ranked by condition, reliability, and driver engagement. No Mazda Miatas surfaced this week within budget; BMW roadsters and a rare MR2 Spyder dominate the podium. Prices range from $7,940 to $13,438.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 2.8 is the sweet spot of the E36/7 generation: BMW's silky inline-6 (192 hp), rear-wheel drive, true roadster proportions, and a proven drivetrain. At $7,940 with only 75K miles, clean title, sold through Private Seller Exchange (buy online, no haggle), this is exceptional value. The 2.8 significantly outdrives the base 1.9 four-cylinder.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top through a full cycle — check motor, latches, and headliner liner. Inspect rear plastic window for yellowing or delamination (replacement is $300–600). Look at rocker panels and floor corners for rust bubbles. Confirm timing chain has been maintained. Check for oil leaks at valve cover and rear main seal (common). Test all electrics including windows and heated seats.
picks.bottomLine: Best raw value roadster in this search — Z3 2.8 inline-6 fun under $8K with low miles is a steal.

picks.whyGood: 11,000 miles on a 2003 car is extraordinary — this is essentially a time capsule Z4. The 2.5i inline-6 is smooth and characterful. At 11K miles the soft top seals, weatherstripping, and interior should all be near original condition. Fred Beans is a reputable dealer group in the area.
picks.whatToVerify: With such low miles, focus on rubber degradation from age (not wear): check all door seals, soft-top seams, and hoses for dry rot. Confirm the car has been started and driven periodically — long-dormant cars have rust in brake rotors and degraded fuel system components. Pull service records. Verify soft top hydraulics operate without hesitation.
picks.bottomLine: A barely-driven Z4 at 11K miles — buy this if the rubber checks out and it drives as fresh as the mileage suggests.

picks.whyGood: The MR2 Spyder is one of the most purely fun cars ever made — mid-engine, lightweight (~2,200 lbs), with a 138-hp 1ZZ-FE that revs freely to 7,000 rpm. 56K miles at $12,500 is fair value. Private Seller Exchange means clean title and no-hassle purchase. Toyota reliability means minimal surprise costs.
picks.whatToVerify: Check the SMT (sequential manual) clutch actuator if equipped — it's the main failure point and rebuild/replacement runs $500–1,500. Inspect the engine bay (mid, behind seats) for oil leaks at valve cover gasket. The soft top is manual-fold and simple — check for tears and zipper condition. MR2 Spyders snap oversteer under throttle-off mid-corner; understand the handling before buying.
picks.bottomLine: Mid-engine perfection at 56K miles — the most fun-per-dollar car in this entire search.

picks.whyGood: A 2014 BMW 428i is genuinely modern: turbocharged 2.0L (240 hp), 8-speed ZF auto, AWD traction, and a proper retractable hardtop that keeps noise and leaks out. At $10,555 (Good Deal) for a 12-year-old 4 Series, this is strong value. The N20 engine is solid with good maintenance history.
picks.whatToVerify: Pull full BMW service history — N20 timing chain tensioner (pre-2015 build dates) was revised under TSB; confirm the update was done. Check for oil leaks at valve cover and around the turbo. Test all electric roof cycle operations. Inspect for rust at the rocker-panel/convertible-top-mount junction. Ask seller for Carfax.
picks.bottomLine: Most capable and weather-ready convertible in the search — modern BMW with AWD and a hardtop at $10.5K is a strong value.

picks.whyGood: 2004 Z4 2.5i at 88K miles for $9,499 is solid value — younger and more refined than the Z3, same inline-6 character, more comfortable interior. No accidents per AutoTrader. Mainline Auto is an established local dealer. The M54 engine in this generation is considered very reliable.
picks.whatToVerify: Check soft-top condition (motor and fabric). M54 engine: inspect for oil leaks at valve cover and VANOS unit. Test cooling system carefully — thermostat and water pump should be preventively replaced around 100K if not documented. Check for corrosion on the top's hydraulic rams.
picks.bottomLine: Strong mid-budget roadster — newer and more refined than a Z3 at a reasonable price point.

picks.whyGood: MINI Cooper S (R57) with the turbocharged 1.6L (172 hp), 65K miles at $8,452. The S variant is meaningfully quicker and more engaging than the base Cooper. No accidents per AutoTrader. The R57 generation is more reliable than the earlier supercharged R53. Good for a fun, nimble daily or weekend car.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect the electric soft top mechanism thoroughly — test repeatedly. R57 S: check for oil leaks at turbo and valve cover. Timing chain tensioner is a known issue on the N14/N18 engine; confirm updated tensioner has been installed. Check coolant system for overheating history. Water intrusion at the trunk when the top seal ages — check the trunk carpet.
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged MINI fun at 65K miles — cheeky character, practical enough for occasional daily use.

picks.whyGood: 44,000 miles on a 1994 Mustang GT convertible is extraordinary — this is a garage-kept example that likely spent its life as a weekend car. The 5.0 V8 (215 hp) offers real torque and V8 sound. SN-95 Mustangs are well-supported with a huge aftermarket. Private seller means no dealer markup. Clean title, no accidents.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible structural flex: press down on each corner — no creaking suggests the torque boxes and subframe connectors are sound. Check convertible top mount-to-rocker junction for rust (common spot). Inspect the 5.0 for oil pan gasket leaks and timing cover leaks. Ask about original vs. replacement top. With 44K miles, confirm all rubber seals are supple not cracked.
picks.bottomLine: The lowest-mileage interesting car in this search — a 32-year-old V8 Mustang with 44K on it is a rare find.

picks.whyGood: The SLK 250 uses Mercedes' Magic Sky Control retractable hardtop — no fabric top, no leaks, completely secure with the roof down or up. The 1.8L turbo (201 hp) is adequate for this weight class. 64K miles at $13,438 (Good Deal) makes this the most refined, weather-proof choice in the top 10. Royal Auto Group is well-reviewed locally.
picks.whatToVerify: Retractable hardtop mechanism: run through 10 cycles, listen for hydraulic hesitation. SLK 250: check for engine mounts (vibration at idle) and the 7G-Tronic transmission service history. Mercedes electronics: confirm all sensors (traction, ABS, SRS) are clear. Rear window seal when top is closed — look for water tracks on rear-seat area carpeting.
picks.bottomLine: Most refined and weather-proof drop-top in this search — hardtop retractable with 64K miles at under $14K.

picks.whyGood: A 2004 Audi A4 Cabriolet with only 45,587 miles is exceptional — well under half the life of this body style. Good Deal rating. The 1.8T is the reliable AWP or AMB variant with years of proven ownership behind it. Audi cabriolets of this era are stylish and well-built; at 45K miles this one barely started its life.
picks.whatToVerify: 1.8T at 45K miles: check for PCV system sludge (common if oil changes were stretched). Confirm timing belt has been done (critical at 75K interval — if it's original at 45K miles, budget for it soon). Soft top: check hydraulic rams for fluid weeping. Inspect for rust at door frame and trunk drain points. Ask for service history.
picks.bottomLine: Very low miles Audi Cabriolet — if the timing belt is fresh this is an excellent low-risk pick.

picks.whyGood: The closest top-10 listing — only 10 miles from Media PA. Under $8K. 2013 MINI Cooper Convertible with 120K miles is well into its lifecycle, but MINIs at this mileage are still roadworthy with attention to maintenance. Fair Deal but the proximity and low price make it a convenient test-drive candidate.
picks.whatToVerify: At 120K miles: confirm timing chain tensioner has been updated, check the electric top for smooth operation, inspect coolant reservoir for cracks (common on N16/N18). Check for water intrusion in the trunk. Soft top seals dry out at this age — budget for replacement. Worth a PPI before purchase given the mileage.
picks.bottomLine: Best proximity pick — 10 miles away, under $8K, fun MINI convertible if the mechanicals check out.

mustangs.introHtml: 5 Ford Mustang convertibles found within 35 miles of Media, PA between $7K–$15K. The 1994 GT with 44,000 miles is the standout — a genuine barn-find-level rarity. Two Fox Bodies, two modern V6 S197s, and a high-mileage 2018 EcoBoost round out the herd.

picks.whyGood: SN-95 Mustang GT with only 44,000 miles — this is a barn find level rarity. The 5.0 V8 makes real V8 sound and torque. Private seller with clean title and no accidents. At 44K miles this car has been coddled. Best Mustang in this search by a wide margin.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible rust check: torque boxes, subframe connectors, and rocker-panel-to-top-mount junction. Test all weather seals. Confirm soft top frame integrity (no bent bows). 5.0: check for timing cover, valve cover, and oil pan leaks. Ask about history — garage kept?
picks.bottomLine: A 44K-mile SN-95 V8 Mustang convertible from a private seller — extremely rare, buy it if it's rust-free.

picks.whyGood: Fox Body Mustang GT — the cult classic. 5.0 HO V8 (225 hp original), lightweight, simple mechanicals with massive aftermarket support. Great Deal badge at $9,500. Only 16 miles from Media PA. Fox Body convertibles are appreciated classics and correct examples are holding value.
picks.whatToVerify: Fox Body convertible rust: check floorboards (especially driver's footwell), rear frame rails, and trunk floor. These are unibody cars with known rust points. Inspect the convertible top frame for corrosion (the metal bows rust through). Check 5.0 for head gasket history and rear main seal leak. If it has T-5 manual, test for third-gear synchro grind.
picks.bottomLine: Fox Body GT at Great Deal pricing — classic American muscle, get a rust check before buying.

picks.whyGood: 2014 S197 Mustang V6 Premium with 79K miles at Good Deal pricing. Premium trim means Shaker audio, MyFord Touch, leather — properly equipped. The 3.7L V6 (305 hp) is actually quick and gets decent fuel economy.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible IRS rear suspension clunk (if equipped): test on rough pavement. Check T-6 automatic fluid (if auto). Inspect the power soft top cycle — listen for motor strain. Check rear seat and trunk for water intrusion from top seal. Confirm no active recalls (check NHTSA by VIN).
picks.bottomLine: Newest Mustang in the search with decent miles and a proper feature list — practical modern pony car.

picks.whyGood: Closest Mustang in the search at only 9 miles from Media PA. S197 V6 at 96K miles. Fair Deal but the proximity makes PPI logistics easy. West Chester is a convenient test drive.
picks.whatToVerify: Same S197 convertible checklist as above. At 96K miles budget for spark plugs (if not done), rear differential fluid, and a soft top inspection. Fair Deal rating means price is average — negotiate.
picks.bottomLine: Most convenient Mustang to inspect — 9 miles away, V6 power, fair price to negotiate.

picks.whyGood: Newest Mustang in the search — 2018 S550 EcoBoost Premium. Modern IRS rear suspension, great handling, latest safety tech. EcoBoost (310 hp) is quick, though purists prefer V8. 141K miles at $13,887 is the concern.
picks.whatToVerify: At 141K miles: check EcoBoost for any turbo whistle or smoke. Inspect the S550 convertible cloth top seal carefully (these are known to shrink in cold weather). CarFax / service history review is essential at this mileage. Confirm brake condition and all fluid services.
picks.bottomLine: Most modern Mustang but highest mileage — buy only with clean Carfax and documented service history.
